name: Reus, Maspujols and Ri
name_original: Maspujols a Riudoms — circular desde Reus
description: This difficult 11.4-mile (18.4 km) jogging route from Reus explores rural landscapes and olive groves between Maspujols and Riudoms.
region_intro: Jogging around Reus offers diverse landscapes, including scenic olive groves and rural areas. The region is characterized by peri-urban streams and ravines, contributing to its natural richness. Many routes provide distant mountain views, enhancing the running experience. The area also features fertile agricultural land with preserved farmhouses, offering a blend of natural and historical elements for runners. Best jogging routes around Reus The most popular jogging route is Passeig de Ronda Trail, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail that takes 44 minutes to complete. This route passes by the Santuari de la Misericòrdia and Parque de Sant Jordi, largely on paved surfaces. Another top favourite among local runners is Reus and Riudoms Circular Trail, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.9 km) path. This route offers picturesque olive groves and mountain views. Local runners also love the Ruta del Carrasclet, PR-C 88 — circular by Institut Pere Mata from Reus, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 57 minutes. Jogging around Reus is defined by olive groves, rural landscapes, and distant mountain views. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging running routes. The routes in Reus are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 1800 runners have used komoot to explore Reus's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Reus?
A: Komoot offers over 160 diverse running routes around Reus, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging trails through the region's unique landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes for beginners in Reus?
A: Yes, Reus has several easy jogging routes suitable for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1122765648/passeig-de-ronda-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Passeig de Ronda Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.1 km route that is largely paved and passes by notable landmarks like Santuari de la Misericòrdia and Parque de Sant Jordi, making it a pleasant option for a lighter run.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails in the Reus area?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a longer challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1135540286/reus-maspujols-and-ri" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reus, Maspujols and Riudoms Circular&lt;/a&gt; offer an extensive 18.4 km journey through rural landscapes and olive groves. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1124912442/puig-d-en-cama-circular-by-gr-72-from-reus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig d'en cama – circular by GR 72&lt;/a&gt;, which is 18.1 km with significant elevation.

Q: Are there circular running routes around Reus?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Reus are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1130911182/reus-and-riudoms-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reus and Riudoms Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10.9 km path offering picturesque olive groves and mountain views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1122911191/ruta-del-carrasclet-pr-c-88-circular-by-institut-pere-mata-from-reus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Carrasclet, PR-C 88&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent circular option.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails in Reus?
A: Jogging routes around Reus offer a rich variety of scenery. You'll encounter scenic &lt;strong&gt;olive groves&lt;/strong&gt;, expansive &lt;strong&gt;rural landscapes&lt;/strong&gt;, and often enjoy &lt;strong&gt;distant mountain views&lt;/strong&gt;. The peri-urban areas also feature natural streams and ravines, alongside fertile agricultural land dotted with preserved farmhouses. Green spaces like Parque de Sant Jordi and Mas Iglesias Park also provide pleasant environments for a run.

Q: Are there any specific parks or green spaces in Reus ideal for jogging?
A: Yes, Reus boasts several parks and green areas perfect for running. &lt;strong&gt;Parque de Sant Jordi&lt;/strong&gt;, located north of the city, is a popular spot and is featured along routes like the Passeig de Ronda Trail. &lt;strong&gt;Mas Iglesias Park&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its rose garden, also offers a pleasant setting for outdoor activity. The city council actively promotes the use of public parks for recreation.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural features to see along the running routes?
A: Many routes offer glimpses of the region's natural and historical charm. You might pass by the Santuari de la Misericòrdia or explore the natural beauty of the Boca de la Mina area, which features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4975838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boca de la Mina Fountain&lt;/a&gt;. For stunning vistas, consider routes that offer views of peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/597960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig d'en Cama (717 m)&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/782537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint over the Cingles de Patxeco&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there shaded running routes in Reus, especially for warmer days?
A: While Reus is known for its sunny climate, some routes offer natural shade. Trails that pass through &lt;strong&gt;olive groves&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;rural landscapes&lt;/strong&gt;, particularly those connecting to areas like Riudoms and Maspujols, may provide some tree cover. Additionally, city parks like Parque de Sant Jordi and Mas Iglesias Park can offer shaded sections within their green spaces.

Q: What do other runners say about jogging in Reus?
A: The running routes in Reus are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, including the picturesque olive groves and distant mountain views, as well as the variety of options available for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there unpaved or natural surface running paths available in Reus?
A: Yes, many routes in Reus venture beyond paved surfaces. The region's &lt;strong&gt;rural landscapes&lt;/strong&gt;, &lt;strong&gt;olive groves&lt;/strong&gt;, and &lt;strong&gt;agricultural land&lt;/strong&gt; provide numerous opportunities for running on natural, unpaved paths. Trails like the Reus and Riudoms Circular Trail or the Ruta del Carrasclet, PR-C 88, will take you through these more natural terrains.

Q: Is it possible to access running trails in Reus using public transport?
A: Reus City Council is actively working on initiatives to improve access to outdoor areas, including creating more park-and-ride spaces on the outskirts. While specific public transport links to every trailhead aren't detailed, many routes are accessible from the city or surrounding towns, making them reachable by local bus services or a short walk from urban centers.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners near Reus?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Reus offers routes with significant elevation and longer distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1124912442/puig-d-en-cama-circular-by-gr-72-from-reus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig d'en cama – circular by GR 72&lt;/a&gt; is an 18.1 km route with over 500 meters of elevation gain, providing a demanding workout. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1135540286/reus-maspujols-and-ri" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reus, Maspujols and Riudoms Circular&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult option at 18.4 km, delving deep into rural landscapes.
tips: The Camí del Mig is an alternative to the PR-C 88 when going between Maspujols and Reus. It's a good trail and offers a bit of variety.
This is a nice narrow trail sometimes through open country and sometimes through reeds. Use this path if you want to go from Riudoms to Les Borges del Camp. It's much nicer than walking on the road.
This is a lovely little park to visit in Riudoms. It has beautiful large trees and the Hermitage of Sant Antoni de Riudoms is the center of it.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like for this jogging route?
faqs.answer: This specific Komoot route, covering 18.4 kilometers with an elevation gain of 155 meters, is classified as difficult and suitable for individuals with very good fitness. The terrain is mostly paved, but you will also encounter sections of dirt paths, especially through olive groves and along the Riera de Maspujols.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead for this circular route?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting from Reus, you can typically find parking within Reus itself, especially near the outskirts where the trail begins. Public transport options to Reus are also readily available, making it convenient to access the starting point without a car.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas, near roads, or in sections where wildlife might be present. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the trail's cleanliness.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this route is during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these seasons, the weather is typically milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler, though often still suitable for jogging on sunny days.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to use this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to jog along this route. It utilizes public paths and trails that are freely accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and points of interest can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse mix of rural and natural landscapes. You'll jog through picturesque fields of olive trees and along the Riera de Maspujols. Notable highlights include El Camí del Mig and the Séquia Path. You'll also pass by the historic Ermita de Sant Antoni de Riudoms. Keep an eye out for distant views of the Muntanyes de Prades and the Sierra de Llabería.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that are part of the Camí de la Riera de Maspujols, Camí dels Molins Nous, and Camí de la Séquia o Camí dels Molinets. This interconnectedness allows for customization if you wish to explore further or shorten your run.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is primarily rural, passing through Maspujols and Riudoms, you will find opportunities for cafes and restrooms within these towns. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the longer stretches between villages.

faqs.question: What is the renaturalization project along the Riera de Maspujols and how does it affect the trail?
faqs.answer: The Riera de Maspujols, a significant part of this route, is undergoing a renaturalization project. This involves removing debris and invasive species, planting native vegetation, and improving signage. These efforts aim to restore the area to a more natural state, enhancing its beauty and ecological value for trail users, with improvements expected to be noticeable by late 2025.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children or strollers?
faqs.answer: Given its classification as a difficult jogging route spanning 18.4 kilometers with some elevation, it is generally not recommended for young children or strollers. However, easier, shorter sections of paths around Maspujols and Riudoms might be suitable for family walks, but not the entirety of this specific circular route.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jogging trip on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this 18.4 km jogging route, we recommend bringing appropriate running shoes, comfortable athletic wear, sufficient water, and snacks. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is advisable, especially during warmer months. A charged phone for navigation and emergencies is also a good idea.

waypoints.name: Ermita de Sant Antoni de Riudoms
